content.text: FishTxt was designed to be a hybrid of a simple notes app and a conventional word document. It is a light-weight text editor that supports both rapid brainstorming
more careful development of drafts. Here’s an example (in fact, the following scenario is precisely the situation that prompted me to design FishTxt).

I was writing an article (~9,000 words) about the history of computation and its conceptual parallels to the history of Idealist philosophy. Now, that’s quite a broad range of topics. Because I am not a genius, I had to develop my draft in fragments, entering a seemingly endless cycle of: write, delete, re-write, drag a bunch of paragraphs, copy & paste, write another paragraph which I’m not sure where to include, put it in my notes app, do more editing, copy and paste that earlier piece into the word doc, and so on.

The blobs are essentially an effort to make such tasks easier. Hence, I should admit that FishTxt is probably not for every writer or researcher out there. It is desgined to specifically help with those who have to juggle between several different topics, sections, fragments, etc., during their work — those who are, like myself, not great at keeping a tidy word document or a notebook.

Really, a blob can be whatever you want it to be: a sentence that you don’t want to forget, a paragraph, a quotation from a journal article along with your comments, a list of topics that you wanna tackle in the future, or even an entire book chapter.
In the current Beta version of the app, the text editor supports the following formatting features, and you can access them through the tool bar at the top.
And headings! Three different levels of headings can be accessed through the “paragraph” button, the first button in the toolbar.
This is a footnote. You can edit it.
